senses.definition: to do something that is not strictly according to rules, often by making exceptions

examples.x: She often bends the rules to give her students more opportunities for creativity.
The company bends the rules occasionally to secure lucrative contracts.
He bent the rules to help his friend avoid a penalty.
The team bent the rules during the game to gain an unfair advantage.
The politician was caught bending the rules and faced severe consequences.
I'm afraid they will bend the rules to meet their sales targets.

senses.definition: in a situation where there is clear proof of one's crime or wrongdoing

examples.x: The detective has the suspect dead to rights with the murder weapon in his possession.
I catch my brother red-handed stealing cookies from the jar, and he knows he's dead to rights.
The auditors are investigating the company's financial records and have found evidence that puts some employees dead to rights.
The prosecution presented the incriminating video footage, putting the defendant dead to rights during the trial.
The undercover agent will gather enough evidence to put the entire smuggling ring dead to rights.
Once the auditors complete their investigation, they are likely to catch several employees dead to rights for embezzlement.

senses.definition: a situation in which one is responsible for the death of a person

examples.x: The corrupt dictator has blood on his hands for ordering the brutal suppression of peaceful protests.
She realizes that her unethical business practices are putting blood on her hands.
The warlord continues to sow violence and has blood on his hands with each innocent life lost.
The ruthless dictator had blood on his hands after years of human rights abuses.
The negligent company executives had blood on their hands when the factory workers suffered a tragic accident.
If the corporation ignores safety regulations, they will have blood on their hands if accidents occur.

senses.definition: a piece of evidence that is used to prove someone's crimes or wrong deeds

examples.x: The detective uncovers a document that is the smoking gun, revealing the suspect's direct involvement in the conspiracy.
The journalist believes she has found the smoking gun, an email that implicates the politician in a corruption scandal.
The prosecutor presents the murder weapon as the smoking gun, providing irrefutable evidence of the defendant's guilt.
The investigators discovered the smoking gun, a surveillance video capturing the suspect committing the crime.
The whistleblower's leaked documents served as the smoking gun that exposed the company's fraudulent activities.

word_list.pronunciation: bˈɪɹd ðə lˈaɪən ɪn hɪz hɜː dˈɛn

senses.definition: to confront someone very powerful or dangerous in their area of control and strength, where they hold the advantage

examples.x: She decides to beard the lion in his den by confronting the powerful CEO about unethical business practices.
They are bearding the lion in his den by challenging the oppressive government in peaceful protests.
The investigative journalist plans to beard the lion in his den by exposing the corrupt politician's misdeeds.
He bearded the lion in his den when he confronted the notorious gang leader in his own territory.
The activists bearded the lion in her den by organizing a protest outside the corporate headquarters.

senses.definition: to publicly oppose a group or party that one has been a member of

examples.x: The soldier decides to break ranks and voice his disagreement with the military strategy.
The politician risks backlash by breaking ranks with their party and advocating for a different approach.
The employee bravely breaks ranks and exposes the company's unethical practices.
She broke ranks with her colleagues and spoke out against the discriminatory policies of the organization.
The athlete who broke ranks and supported a rival team faced criticism from their former teammates.
The diplomat broke ranks with their government and revealed classified information to the public.
If the negotiations fail, the country might break ranks and pursue its own course of action.
The labor union threatens to break ranks and go on strike if their demands are not met.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈeɪk ðə lˈɔː ˌɪntʊ wˈʌnz ˈoʊn ɔːɹ hˈændz

senses.definition: to illegally punish someone in a way that one personally thinks is right or just, often by the use of violence

examples.x: The frustrated citizens feel tempted to take the law into their own hands and confront the local gang.
She decides to take the law into her own hands by conducting her own investigation to find her sister's killer.
The vigilante group believes they are justified in taking the law into their own hands to protect the neighborhood.
They took the law into their own hands and administered punishment to the thief before the authorities arrived.
The rancher, tired of cattle theft, took the law into his own hands and set up traps to catch the culprits.
In the absence of effective law enforcement, the townspeople took the law into their own hands to restore order.
If the legal system fails to deliver justice, some individuals may feel compelled to take the law into their own hands.
The community is concerned that desperate circumstances might lead some to take the law into their own hands.
The activist group plans to take the law into their own hands if the government continues to ignore their demands.
The community, frustrated with rising crime rates, took the law into their hands and formed their own neighborhood watch.
In the absence of effective law enforcement, some individuals resort to taking the law into their hands and seeking personal justice.
The victim's family, feeling failed by the legal system, decided to take the law into their hands and hire a private investigator.

senses.definition: to ignore red traffic lights and move past them

examples.x: He often jumps the light and risks getting a traffic ticket.
She frequently jumps the light when she's in a hurry.
They always remind their teenage son not to jump the light while driving.
Yesterday, he jumped the light and narrowly avoided colliding with another car.
She regretted having jumped the light because it resulted in a hefty fine.
They got pulled over by the police after they jumped the light last week.
If he continues to jump the light, he will eventually cause a serious accident.
She promises never to jump the light again after her close call yesterday.
They will face severe consequences if they jump the light during their driving exam.

senses.definition: used to refer to a situation in which people are willing to do whatever it takes to succeed

examples.x: In the competitive business world, it's the law of the jungle, where only the strongest survive.
He operates by the law of the jungle, always looking out for his own interests without regard for others.
The political landscape is governed by the law of the jungle, with politicians vying for power and influence.
During the chaotic period, the country was ruled by the law of the jungle, with rampant corruption and violence.
In the early days of the industry, it was every company for itself, following the law of the jungle.
The team learned the hard way that in the world of professional sports, it's the law of the jungle, and only the fittest teams can survive.
If we don't establish fair regulations and guidelines, society may devolve into the law of the jungle.
As resources become scarce, the law of the jungle will prevail, and individuals will fight for survival.
Without proper governance, the digital realm may descend into the law of the jungle, with no protection for users' rights or privacy.

senses.definition: a place in which rules and laws are not followed or obeyed

examples.x: The outskirts of the city are notorious for being bandit territory, where the absence of law enforcement allows lawlessness to prevail.
If the situation worsens, that region might become a full-fledged bandit territory, where rules and laws will be completely ignored or disobeyed.
Without proper intervention, the area could potentially transform into bandit territory, leading to a breakdown of order and a disregard for rules and laws.

senses.definition: to try to unfairly move past a group of people lining up somewhere before one's turn arrives

examples.x: The impatient customer tries to jump the line, causing frustration among the other waiting customers.
He always jumps the line at the movie theater, thinking he can get away with it.
People who jump the line at the grocery store often face disapproving looks from fellow shoppers.

word_list.word: off the back of a truck

senses.definition: used for saying that a free or unreasonably cheap product is not obtained legally

examples.x: He often buys goods off the back of a truck, even though he knows they might be stolen.
The street vendor is currently selling merchandise off the back of a truck, attracting curious customers.
People sometimes purchase electronics off the back of a truck, unaware of their questionable origin.
Last week, he bought a TV off the back of a truck, only to find out it was defective and likely stolen.
If you're not careful, you might end up buying stolen goods off the back of a truck without even knowing it.

senses.definition: (of a person) not attending a place one was supposed to or leaving an obligation without any notice or permission
an unannounced and unauthorized absence from a job, workplace, or other commitment

examples.x: The company plans to implement stricter policies to discourage employees from taking French leave.

senses.definition: to secretly put a drug in a person's drink in order to make them unconscious
a vivid and often bizarre hallucination that a person may experience, typically as a result of heavy drinking or drug use

examples.x: He often sees pink elephants whenever he drinks too much.
They laughingly discuss the idea of pink elephants while enjoying a few drinks together.
Last night, he saw pink elephants after consuming a few too many cocktails at the party.
They jokingly reminisced about the time they thought they were seeing pink elephants during their college days.
If he keeps drinking excessively, he will likely continue to see pink elephants in the future.

senses.definition: at a time when it is illegal to hunt an animal

examples.x: It is currently out of season to hunt deer in this region.
The regulations state that it is illegal to fish for trout out of season.
Last year, they were fined for hunting ducks out of season.
